inner-join.label: INNER JOIN
inner-join.summary: Retrieve matching rows from two tables.

sketch-what-is-inner-join.title: What is an INNER JOIN?
sketch-what-is-inner-join.bodyMarkdown: An **INNER JOIN** in SQL is used to combine rows from two tables based on a related column between them. Only rows with matching values in both tables are included in the result set. This is the most common type of join.

sketch-inner-join-syntax.title: INNER JOIN Syntax
sketch-inner-join-syntax.bodyMarkdown:
The basic syntax for an INNER JOIN is:

```sql
SELECT columns
FROM table1
INNER JOIN table2
  ON table1.column = table2.column;
```

You specify which columns to match using the `ON` clause.

quiz-inner-join-basic-concept.title: Understanding INNER JOIN
quiz-inner-join-basic-concept.prompt: What does an INNER JOIN do in SQL?
quiz-inner-join-basic-concept.hint: Think about which rows are included when using INNER JOIN.

help.concept: INNER JOIN returns only rows where there is a match in both tables based on the join condition.
help.hint_1: It does not include unmatched rows from either table.
help.hint_2: Only rows with matching values in the joined columns are returned.

options.a: Returns all rows from both tables, matching or not.
options.b: Returns only rows with matching values in both tables.
options.c: Returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right table.
options.d: Returns all rows from the right table and matching rows from the left table.

quiz-inner-join-syntax-order.title: Arrange the INNER JOIN Syntax
quiz-inner-join-syntax-order.prompt: Put the parts of an INNER JOIN query in the correct order.
quiz-inner-join-syntax-order.hint: Start with SELECT, then FROM, then the JOIN, then the ON clause.

help.concept: SQL queries follow a specific order: SELECT, FROM, JOIN, ON.
help.hint_1: The ON clause always comes after the JOIN.
help.hint_2: The FROM clause comes right after SELECT.

quiz-inner-join-fill-blank.title: Fill in the INNER JOIN Keyword
quiz-inner-join-fill-blank.prompt:
Complete the SQL statement to join two tables:

`SELECT * FROM customers ____ orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id;`
quiz-inner-join-fill-blank.hint: Which keyword joins only matching rows from both tables?

help.concept: The the missing term keyword is used to select only rows with matching values in both tables.
help.hint_1: LEFT JOIN and RIGHT JOIN include unmatched rows, but the missing term does not.
help.hint_2: The most basic join for matching rows is the missing term.

quiz-inner-join-fill-blank.template: SELECT * FROM customers ____ orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id;

quiz-inner-join-code-input.title: Write an INNER JOIN Query
quiz-inner-join-code-input.prompt: Using the `products_catalog` dataset, write a SQL query to select the product name and category name for all products, using an INNER JOIN between the `products` and `categories` tables. The `products` table has a `category_id` column that matches the `id` column in `categories`.
quiz-inner-join-code-input.hint: Join the two tables using the category ID fields.

help.concept: Use INNER JOIN to combine rows from both tables where the category IDs match.
help.hint_1: Select the product name from products and the category name from categories.
help.hint_2: Use ON to specify products.category_id = categories.id.
